generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary heat source delivering ~100W power to warm air in the thermal incubator system. Controlled via Raspberry Pi 4 Model B through GPIO and optocouplers for current separation (4A capacity handled by MOSFETs)."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Circulates air at 0.02 m³/s airflow to achieve uniform temperature distribution in the styrofoam insulated container. Operates in 2-second full-cycle time."
    DTDFVocab:producedData FanStatusDataTransmitted
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance RoomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External DS18S20 sensor monitoring room temperature (293K-300K range) with ±0.5°C accuracy (-10°C to +85°C range)."
]

instance RoomAirTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 sensor measuring air temperature in the room (T1 position) with ±0.5°C accuracy (-10°C to +85°C range)."
]

instance HottestAirTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 sensor measuring hottest air temperature (T2 position) with ±0.5°C accuracy (-10°C to +85°C range)."
]

instance ColdestAirTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 sensor measuring coldest air temperature (T3 position) with ±0.5°C accuracy (-10°C to +85°C range)."
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance RoomTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RoomTemperatureSensor
]

instance RoomAirTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RoomAirTemperatureSensor
]

instance HottestAirTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HottestAirTemperatureSensor
]

instance ColdestAirTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ColdestAirTemperatureSensor
]

instance FanStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FanActuator
]

instance HeatbedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HeatbedActuator
]

instance PowerInputDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Voltage (V) and current (I) measurements from power supply interface"
]

// C5: Data
instance RoomTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Experimental room temperature readings (T_room) from external DS18S20 sensor used for model calibration and uniformity validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RoomTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance RoomAirTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature readings (T_bair) from room air sensor at T1 position used for thermal model validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RoomAirTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance HottestAirTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature readings (T2) from hottest air sensor used to validate thermal uniformity assumption"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HottestAirTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance ColdestAirTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature readings (T3) from coldest air sensor used to validate thermal uniformity assumption"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ColdestAirTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance FanStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "On/off status of the circulating fan used for system state monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FanStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance HeatbedStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "On/off status of the heatbed actuator used for system state monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HeatbedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance PowerInputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Voltage (V) and current (I) measurements from power supply interface used for energy flow modeling"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PowerInputDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance ExperimentalTrajectoriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Experimental temperature trajectories (figs. 10-12) used for non-linear least squares calibration of thermal models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ScipyEnabler
]

instance CalibrationParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized model parameters (C_air, C_heater, G_heater, G_box) from Scipy least-squares solver"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelB
]

// C10: Models
instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Single-parameter model (C_air) describing energy flow between air and room (eq. 5), assuming all power goes to air"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler, ScipyEnabler
]

instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Four-parameter model (C_air, C_heater, G_heater, G_box) accounting for heatbed thermal inertia (eqs. 8-9). Preferred for thermal inertia prediction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler, ScipyEnabler, TemperatureRegulationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asynchronous message broker (RabbitMQ) on Raspberry Pi that periodically reads temperature data from 1-Wire bus and uploads it to DT"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance ScipyEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based least-squares solver (Scipy) for non-linear parameter optimization using experimental trajectories (eq. 11)"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]

instance StatechartControllerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Statechart-based bang-bang controller implementing hysteresis (LL/UL) and delay compensation (H/C) logic for temperature regulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureRegulationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Periodic transmission of temperature data (T1, T2, T3) from sensors to RabbitMQ for model calibration and monitoring"
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Calibration of thermal models (Model A/B) using non-linear least squares to minimize residual error between simulated and experimental trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:provides CalibrationParametersData
]

instance TemperatureRegulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic temperature regulation via bang-bang controller with hysteresis and delay compensation for optimal temperature distribution"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlStrategyActions
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of temperature profiles (T1, T2, T3) and system states (heatbed/fan status) via experimental results (figs. 10-12)"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureUniformityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance CalibrationParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calibrated model parameters (C_air, C_heater, G_heater, G_box) improve predictive accuracy of thermal models"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CalibrationParametersData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelB
]

instance ControlStrategyActions : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Bang-bang controller decisions (H/C delays) optimize temperature regulation via statechart-based logic with hysteresis and delay compensation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureUniformityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Temperature uniformity measurements (figs. 10-12) inform fan performance adjustments and validate Assumption 1"
]

instance ModelBasedOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Future implementation of PID control strategy for more sophisticated temperature regulation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment
instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, RoomTemperatureSensor,
              RoomAirTemperatureSensor, HottestAirTemperatureSensor,
              ColdestAirTemperatureSensor, StyrofoamInsulationComponent
]

instance StyrofoamInsulationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated styrofoam container (0.03 m³ volume) housing the heatbed and fan for thermal regulation"
]

// C13: Physical Components
instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B serving as central controller interfacing with GPIO, RabbitMQ, and DS18S20 sensors"
]

instance PowerSupplyComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Power supply interfacing with heatbed and fan actuators (12V/18V) with current handling via MOSFETs/optocouplers"
]

instance PCBComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "PCB design containing MOSFETs and optocouplers for current separation (4A heatbed current handling)"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT communicates with the PT via **RabbitMQ** to issue control commands:
  - **Outputs**: Commands to the **heatbed** (on/off) and **fan** (voltage adjustment) through the low-level driver.
  - **Actuation**: The Raspberry Pi’s GPIO controls the heatbed/fan via MOSFETs/optocouplers, ensuring safe current separation (4A handled by hardware). The controller implements a **bang-bang strategy** with parameters: LL (lower limit), UL (upper limit), H (heating duration), C (waiting duration)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on **real-time and near-real-time scales**:
  - **Sensing Rate**: Temperature data is sampled periodically (~every few seconds) via the low-level driver.
  - **Model Update Rate**: Calibration parameters are optimized offline (experimental data trajectories) using Scipy’s gradient descent (iterative least-squares).
  - **Control Cycle**: The bang-bang controller executes in discrete steps (H/C delays) tied to physical actuation (e.g., 2-second heating/waiting intervals). Synchronization between DT and PT occurs via RabbitMQ message latency (~ms–s)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is **decentralized and modular**:
  - **Components**:
    1. **Physical Twin (PT)**: Insulated container, heatbed, fan, sensors, Raspberry Pi.
    2. **Digital Twin (DT)**:
       - **Calibration Module**: Optimizes Model A/B parameters using experimental data.
       - **Monitoring Module**: Processes temperature data (T_bair, T_room) from RabbitMQ.
       - **Control Module**: Implements bang-bang controller (LL, UL, H, C) via GPIO.
  - **Orchestration**: RabbitMQ acts as the central message broker, decoupling PT (hardware) from DT (software) components. The DT is implemented as a **service** that listens to RabbitMQ messages (fig. 7)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans **three lifecycle phases**:
  1. **Design Phase**: Ideal model development (Model A/B) with assumptions (e.g., uniform temperature, negligible heatbed heat retention).
  2. **Manufactured Phase**: Physical validation via experiments (uniformity tests, calibration).
  3. **Operated Phase**: Real-time monitoring and control via the Raspberry Pi controller (RabbitMQ communication). The DT represents the **operated** twin of the PT, with models updated via calibration."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are addressed via:
  - **Verification**:
    - Assumption 1 (uniformity) validated via experiments (max 7°C/6°C discrepancy at 12V/18V fan).
    - Model calibration minimizes residual error (Scipy least-squares) between simulated and experimental trajectories.
  - **Validation**:
    - Model B outperforms Model A by capturing heatbed thermal inertia (four parameters vs. one).
    - Assumptions 2–4 are tested experimentally (e.g., negligible heatbed heat absorption).
  - **Uncertainty**:
    - Temperature sensors (±0.5°C accuracy) and model parameters (C_air, C_heater, G_heater, G_box) are calibrated iteratively.
    - Assumptions (e.g., constant specific heat capacity) introduce simplifications."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT connects to the PT via:
  - **Network Protocol**: RabbitMQ (asynchronous message broker) for real-time data exchange.
  - **Communication Interface**:
    - **Sensing**: 1-Wire bus (DS18S20 sensors) → Raspberry Pi GPIO → RabbitMQ.
    - **Actuation**: GPIO → MOSFETs/optocouplers → Heatbed/Fan → PT.
  - **Latency**: Message processing occurs within milliseconds, ensuring low-latency control."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted on the **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** as a local deployment:
  - **Hardware**: Single-board computer (GPIO, Wi-Fi/Bluetooth) for real-time control.
  - **Software Stack**:
    - RabbitMQ server (in-memory message broker).
    - Low-level driver (Python/C) for sensor/actuator abstraction.
    - Control logic (statechart-based) and calibration scripts (Scipy).
  - **Scalability**: Open-source repository allows cloud/on-premises deployment (e.g., Dockerized RabbitMQ)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The DT includes:
  - **Safety**:
    - Hardware isolation: MOSFETs/optocouplers prevent Raspberry Pi from handling 4A current.
    - Control logic: Bang-bang controller with LL/UL limits prevents overheating.
  - **Cybersecurity**:
    - RabbitMQ is deployed locally (Raspberry Pi) to mitigate network exposure.
    - No explicit encryption mentioned; assumes secure local network deployment.
  - **Human Safety**: Remote actuation is not enabled; all control is local to the Raspberry Pi."
]
